---
title: "Next.js Templates"
description: "Review the initial Next.js template families StackLab plans to document and ship."
---

# Next.js Templates

Next.js is the strongest first template family for StackLab because it supports:

- content and docs
- landing pages
- dashboards
- full-stack app-router products
- strong SEO and server-rendering paths

## Planned Template Categories

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="Landing">
    Fast product marketing surface with strong layout primitives and a docs-aware information model.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="SEO-first">
    Content-heavy site optimized for discoverability, page metadata, and structured information architecture.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Dashboard">
    Auth-aware application shell for admin tools, SaaS dashboards, or internal tools.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Realtime">
    Collaboration- or event-driven starter with clear boundaries for subscriptions and live updates.
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Documentation Standard

Each template page should explain:

- when to choose that template
- what it optimizes for
- what it intentionally does not include
- where scale or complexity changes the architecture
